---
title: "Mary Arthur"
---

## Mary Arthur

### Description

missionary

### Biography

Mentioned, as a missionary who came to South Africa in 1861-2 and looked after orphans, by A.E.M. Anderson Morshead, <i>A Pioneer and Founder: Reminiscences of Some who Knew Robert Gray D D, First Bishop of Cape Town</i> (London: Skeffington 1905), and presumably the Miss Arthur referred to by CMY (6 Dec 1866).  See \'The Ladies\' Association, or, Great Causes Helped by Small Means\' <i>MP</i> (May 1867) 510:  \'Little native girls may also be maintained in such boarding-schools as the Missions possess.  Such may be found at Miss Arthur\'s, at the Cape, where the cost is £6 per annum\'.

See the website of the institution today at http://www.st-georges.org.za/about.html

### Letters

- Letter from October 8, 1869.
- Letter from Dec 6th [1866]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
